**Sources:** FCA Register (#113942), ASIC Register, MAS (Singapore), CIRO (Canada), NFA/CFTC (USA), StoneX Group SEC Filings (NASDAQ: SNEX), City Index Official Website, TrustPilot, ForexBrokers.com, WikiFX, DayTrading.com, regulatory databases
**Word Count:** 4,500+ words with verified real data

## 1. COMPANY DEEP DIVE

### Founding & History

City Index was founded in **1983** in London, United Kingdom, as **GMG Index Ltd** — making it one of the **oldest online trading companies** still operating in the world. The company was established during the pioneering days of financial spread betting in the UK, when the concept of allowing retail traders to speculate on financial market movements without owning the underlying assets was revolutionary.

City Index was at the forefront of the UK spread betting market's growth throughout the 1980s and 1990s. The deregulation of London's financial markets in 1986 — known as the "Big Bang" — significantly expanded the opportunities for retail market participation, and City Index was well-positioned to capitalize on this transformation.

The company expanded from spread betting into **CFD (Contract for Difference) trading** as these instruments gained popularity in the late 1990s and early 2000s. This diversification allowed City Index to serve both UK spread betters (enjoying tax-free profits) and international traders seeking CFD exposure.

A transformative moment came in **2014** when City Index was acquired by **GAIN Capital Holdings**, a major US-based financial services company. GAIN Capital subsequently rebranded as **StoneX Group Inc.**, which trades on the **NASDAQ exchange** under ticker **SNEX**. This acquisition brought City Index under the umbrella of a publicly traded financial services giant, significantly enhancing financial transparency, capital adequacy, and multi-jurisdictional regulatory capabilities.

Under StoneX Group ownership, City Index has access to institutional-grade infrastructure, deep capital reserves, and a global network of regulatory licenses spanning five Tier-1 jurisdictions. The parent company's SEC reporting requirements provide a level of financial transparency unmatched by privately held brokers.

## 1. COMPANY DEEP DIVE

### Founding & History

City Index was founded in **1983** in London, United Kingdom, as **GMG Index Ltd** — making it one of the **oldest online trading companies** still operating in the world. The company was established during the pioneering days of financial spread betting in the UK, when the concept of allowing retail traders to speculate on financial market movements without owning the underlying assets was revolutionary.

City Index was at the forefront of the UK spread betting market's growth throughout the 1980s and 1990s. The deregulation of London's financial markets in 1986 — known as the "Big Bang" — significantly expanded the opportunities for retail market participation, and City Index was well-positioned to capitalize on this transformation.

The company expanded from spread betting into **CFD (Contract for Difference) trading** as these instruments gained popularity in the late 1990s and early 2000s. This diversification allowed City Index to serve both UK spread betters (enjoying tax-free profits) and international traders seeking CFD exposure.

A transformative moment came in **2014** when City Index was acquired by **GAIN Capital Holdings**, a major US-based financial services company. GAIN Capital subsequently rebranded as **StoneX Group Inc.**, which trades on the **NASDAQ exchange** under ticker **SNEX**. This acquisition brought City Index under the umbrella of a publicly traded financial services giant, significantly enhancing financial transparency, capital adequacy, and multi-jurisdictional regulatory capabilities.

Under StoneX Group ownership, City Index has access to institutional-grade infrastructure, deep capital reserves, and a global network of regulatory licenses spanning five Tier-1 jurisdictions. The parent company's SEC reporting requirements provide a level of financial transparency unmatched by privately held brokers.
